summary: Core cleanup logic is sound with good dataclass structure and argument handling. However, test assertions are embedded directly in workflow YAML (lines ~55–125), creating tight coupling between test expectations and pipeline definition. The --dry-run flag is unused (cleanup.py line 82: `dry_run = not args.execute` makes the flag irrelevant due to `action='store_true'`). Repetitive JSON load/assert patterns in workflow (4x) should be factored into a helper script. Minimal error handling—JSONDecodeError crashes ungracefully, no validation of input structure. Index-based artifact tracking (artifact_cleanup.py lines 53–63) adds unnecessary complexity. Would function in act but violates maintainability: changing test expectations requires editing YAML.
